figures.caption: Figure 2: The TOPCELL workflow, showing Data Preparation (Left) to build a topology-diverse dataset and a GNN model for routability reward, and the main process (Right), which includes GRPO Post-Training (Top) to update the LLM policy and Inference (Bottom) to optimize a given cell topology.
Figure 3: Structure of the Swap Region in LLM-Guided Topology Permutation. The region is bounded by NCA and NCD.

Table 2: Comparison of layout quality and runtime between SO3-Cell and TOPCELL. TOPCELL generates optimized netlists in only a fraction of the time required by SO3-Cell, achieving comparable layout quality with an average speedup of 85.91×.

Table 1: Comparative analysis of model performance in the evaluation benchmark. The overall SOTA results are shown in bold. Numbers in red indicate the performance improvement of our fine-tuned TOPCELLmodels over the respective Qwen2.5-Coder base models.

Figure 1: Comparison of 7𝑛𝑚 AOI221_X1 layouts before (Top) and after (Bottom) topology optimization by TOPCELL. The optimized topology doesn’t require a dummy gate insertion, leading to a smaller area. This demonstrates the effectiveness of TOPCELL for improving cell layout quality.
